remember: Recall the general properties of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
Identify the different types of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
List the characteristic reactions of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
understand: Explain the structure and bonding in aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
Describe the formation of aldehydes and ketones from carboxylic acids.
Understand the mechanism of nucleophilic addition reactions of aldehydes and ketones.
apply: Use the chemical tests to distinguish between aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
Apply the knowledge of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids to synthesize new compounds.
Use the properties of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids to predict their reactivity.
analyze: Analyze the effect of substituents on the physical and chemical properties of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
Compare the reactivity of aldehydes and ketones towards nucleophilic addition reactions.
Evaluate the importance of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids in industry and daily life.

ncert_objectives: To study the general properties and reactions of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
To understand the structure and bonding in aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
To explain the formation of aldehydes and ketones from carboxylic acids.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the functional groups of aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
To describe the chemical tests for distinguishing between aldehydes, ketones and carboxylic acids.
To explain the mechanism of aldol condensation reaction.
